steps.title: Use the double root condition
steps.content: A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. For the equation $$x^2+\sqrt{k-5}x+35=0$$, the coefficient values are $a=1$, $$b=\sqrt{k-5}$$, and $c=35$.

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content:
Set the discriminant equal to $0$. $$ (\sqrt{k-5})^2-4(1)(35)=0 $$ $$ k-5-140=0 $$ $$ k=145 $$
The answer is A.
